chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C.P No. D – 1590 of 2021 Date Order with signature of Judge Fresh Case 1.For orders on CMA 7689/21 2.For orders on office objection 3.For orders on CMA 7690/21 4.For hearing of main case. 5.For orders on CMA 7691/21 16-11-2021 Mr. Shewak Ram Valeecha, Advocate for Petitioner..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Through instant petition, the Petitioner is seeking her employment in Respondents Department on the basis of plot donation. The said controversy was decided by the Hon’ble Supreme Court in Civil Appeals No. 19-K to 50-K of 2015, re: Government of Sindh and others V/S Loung Khan Rajper and others Vide Order dated 09.11.2015, wherein, it has been held that such appointments cannot be allowed on plot donation basis. In view of such position, instant Petition being misconceived and not maintainable is hereby dismissed in limine with pending applications. Judge Judge Ahmad

In view of such position, instant Petition being misconceived and not maintainable is hereby dismissed in limine with pending applications. Judge Judge Ahmad ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C.P No. D – 1618 of 2021 Date Order with signature of Judge Fresh Case 1.For orders on CMA 7829/21 2.For orders on office objection 3.For orders on CMA 7830/21 4.For hearing of main case. 16-11-2021 Irshad Ahmed Bhutto, the Petitioner present in person..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Through instant petition, the petitioner is seeking his employment in respondents department on the basis of plot donation. However, learned AAG has pointed out that the Hon’ble Supreme Court of Pakistan has decided the said controversy in Civil Appeals No. 19-K to 50-K of 2015, re: Government of Sindh and others V/S Loung Khan Rajper and others Vide Order dated 09.11.2015, wherein, it has been held that such appointments cannot be allowed on plot donation basis. Since the petitioner and his counsel are not in attendance, therefore, this petition stands dismissed for Non-Prosecution. Judge Judge Ahmad
